title: leucanthemum vulgare var. amplifolium
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare var. amplifolium, also known as the large-leaved oxeye daisy, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The large-leaved oxeye daisy has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, due to its rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Leucanthemum vulgare var. amplifolium can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional remedies to promote healing and well-being.
